title: ASTRAGALUS POLYSACCHARIDES
description: Astragalus polysaccharides (APS) are bioactive compounds extracted from Astragalus membranaceus, a traditional Chinese medicinal herb. These complex carbohydrates work primarily through immunomodulation, activating macrophages, T-cells, and natural killer cells while regulating inflammatory cytokines and influencing gut microbiota composition. They are primarily studied for immune enhancement, anti-inflammatory effects, and metabolic health benefits.

research.title: Structural characterization and anti-inflammatory activity of polysaccharides from Astragalus membranaceus
research.summary: Two polysaccharides (APS-A1 and APS-B1) were isolated and characterized, demonstrating anti-inflammatory properties with distinct molecular structures and compositions.

research.title: Effects of Dietary Astragalus Polysaccharide Supplementation on the Th17/Treg Balance and the Gut Microbiota of Broiler Chickens Challenged With Necrotic Enteritis
research.summary: APS supplementation significantly improved immune function by modulating Th17/Treg balance, reducing inflammatory cytokines, and favorably altering gut microbiota composition in disease-challenged subjects.

research.title: Functional fractions of Astragalus polysaccharides as a potential prebiotic to alleviate ulcerative colitis
research.summary: Specific APS fractions demonstrated prebiotic effects and therapeutic potential for ulcerative colitis by modulating gut microbiota and reducing intestinal inflammation.

research.title: Astragalus polysaccharides ameliorate osteoarthritis via inhibiting apoptosis by regulating ROS-mediated ASK1/p38 MAPK signaling pathway targeting on TXN
research.summary: APS protected chondrocytes from apoptosis and promoted collagen II expression by reducing oxidative stress through the ASK1/p38 MAPK pathway, showing promise for osteoarthritis treatment.

research.title: Polysaccharides derived from Astragalus membranaceus and Glycyrrhiza uralensis improve growth performance of broilers by enhancing intestinal health and modulating gut microbiota
research.summary: Combined polysaccharides from Astragalus and Glycyrrhiza improved growth performance, intestinal barrier function, and beneficial gut bacteria populations.

research.title: Astragalus polysaccharides repress myocardial lipotoxicity in a PPARalpha-dependent manner in vitro and in vivo in mice
research.summary: APS demonstrated cardioprotective effects by modulating lipid metabolism through PPARα pathways, reducing myocardial lipotoxicity associated with diabetic disorders.

research.title: Immunomodulatory effect of γ-irradiated Astragalus polysaccharides on immunosuppressed broilers
research.summary: Gamma-irradiated APS enhanced immune function in immunosuppressed subjects, with modified physicochemical properties potentially improving bioactivity.

research.title: Comparative evaluation of polysaccharides isolated from Astragalus, oyster mushroom, and yacon as inhibitors of α-glucosidase
research.summary: Astragalus polysaccharides demonstrated α-glucosidase inhibitory activity, suggesting potential applications in blood sugar management and diabetes prevention.

sentiment.summary: Users show moderate interest in Astragalus as part of comprehensive health stacks, particularly for immune support and longevity protocols. While it appears in many supplement regimens without reported issues, there is limited standalone enthusiasm or dramatic effect reports compared to other supplements.

effects.label: Synergistic Effects:
effects.description: Most effective when combined with other adaptogens (rhodiola, ginseng) or immune-supporting compounds rather than as standalone supplement

effects.label: Preventive Benefits:
effects.description: Better suited for long-term immune support and disease prevention rather than acute treatment of existing conditions

effects.label: Individual Variation:
effects.description: Effects appear subtle and gradual, with benefits more noticeable in comprehensive wellness protocols than single-supplement use

effects.label: Research Translation:
effects.description: Strong animal study results but limited dramatic user reports suggest potential gaps between laboratory findings and real-world human experiences

dosage.label: Standard Dose:
dosage.description: 200-900mg daily commonly used in supplement stacks, often taken continuously

dosage.label: Extract Concentration:
dosage.description: 550mg appears frequently in commercial products, with polysaccharide content varying by brand

dosage.label: Cycling Approach:
dosage.description: Some users rotate adaptogens including Astragalus every other day to prevent tolerance

dosage.label: Combined Formulas:
dosage.description: Often found in 150mg doses when combined with other herbs like Glycyrrhiza in commercial blends

sideEffects.label: Emotional Sensitivity:
sideEffects.description: Rare reports of increased tearfulness or emotional responses when combined with other adaptogens

sideEffects.label: Minimal Direct Effects:
sideEffects.description: Very few standalone side effects reported, suggesting good general tolerability

sideEffects.label: Quality Concerns:
sideEffects.description: Some users warn about product quality variation, particularly with unverified brands on online marketplaces

sideEffects.label: Interaction Potential:
sideEffects.description: Limited negative reports when stacked with multiple other supplements, though comprehensive testing not widely documented

availability.label: Widely Available:
availability.description: Commonly found in health stores and online retailers, often in combination formulas

availability.label: Brand Quality:
availability.description: Users emphasize importance of reputable brands, with some concern about efficacy of cheaper options

availability.label: Extract Forms:
availability.description: Available as standardized polysaccharide extracts and raw root powder, with extract forms preferred for consistent dosing
